message:
fix(workflows): truncate_for_log must snap to a UTF-8 char boundary

Fixes a potential panic in `truncate_for_log` when the input contains
multi-byte UTF-8 codepoints (Cyrillic, CJK, emoji) and `max_len - 3`
lands in the middle of a codepoint. The prior implementation did:

    format!("{}...", &s[..max_len.saturating_sub(3)])

which, for `truncate_for_log("🎉ab", 5)`, sliced to byte 2 — inside
the 4-byte sequence for 🎉 — and panicked with "byte index is not a
char boundary".

This matters because `truncate_for_log` is called on arbitrary pane
output in `no_prompt_detected(last=…)` (wait_execution.rs around
line 621). Pane output can carry adversarial-or-accidental UTF-8 via
the user's terminal, so an unchecked byte-slice here is a workflow-
runner crash vector on unlucky terminal content.

## Fix

Walk `boundary` down from `max_len - 3` (clamped to `s.len()`) until
it is a char boundary, then slice up to that position and append the
ellipsis. This preserves the prior byte-budget contract as closely
as possible — on ASCII input the output is byte-identical to before;
on multi-byte input we cut at the nearest lower char boundary, which
can only make the output SHORTER, never longer, so no log budget is
ever exceeded.

## Test coverage

Four new regression tests, each targeting a distinct failure mode:

- `truncate_for_log_does_not_panic_on_emoji_at_boundary` — the
  exact `"🎉ab", 5` reproducer. Asserts no panic and that the output
  does not introduce `\u{FFFD}` replacement chars.
- `truncate_for_log_does_not_panic_on_cyrillic_at_boundary` —
  walks every max_len from 4..=19 on "привет мир" (19 bytes) to
  exercise every possible mid-char boundary for 2-byte sequences.
- `truncate_for_log_does_not_panic_on_cjk_at_boundary` — walks
  every max_len from 0..=20 on "日本語テスト" (18 bytes, 3-byte
  codepoints).
- `truncate_for_log_max_len_below_ellipsis_emits_ellipsis_only` —
  pins the existing (acceptable) behavior that the ellipsis may
  exceed a tiny max_len, so a future "micro-budget" refactor does
  not silently change what shows up in logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
